planName: frai.tv SEO + YouTube Cross-Linking Implementation Plan
summary: Complete technical plan to make frai.tv crawlable by Google, add structured data, fix caching, and create a bidirectional SEO flywheel between frai.tv and YouTube @remAIke_IT

currentProblems.zeroIndexation: Not a single page indexed by Google as of 2026-04-10
currentProblems.spaRendering: Pure React SPA - Googlebot sees only
currentProblems.noStructuredData: Zero Schema.org / JSON-LD on any page
currentProblems.noOgTags: No OpenGraph or Twitter Card meta tags
currentProblems.antiCacheHeaders: index.html has no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate which prevents any CDN/proxy caching
currentProblems.flatSitemap: 437 URLs but all with same lastmod, no video sitemap extensions, missing navigation pages

phase1_prerendering.title: Make frai.tv crawlable - Pre-rendering Solution
phase1_prerendering.recommendation: vite-plugin-ssg (Static Site Generation at build time)
reasoning: All video data is bundled in remaikeData.js (~436 videos) - no runtime API needed for core content
SSG is simpler than SSR because there is no dynamic user-specific content on public pages
Alternatives rejected: vite-plugin-ssr (now Vike) adds server runtime complexity; prerender.io costs money; react-snap is unmaintained; Cloudflare Workers requires rewriting rendering logic

step1_install.notes: If vite-plugin-ssg does not support createBrowserRouter, use react-snap as fallback or switch to a custom prerender script using Playwright (already in devDependencies)

step2_custom_prerender_script.description: Since createBrowserRouter is incompatible with most SSG plugins, build a custom Playwright-based prerenderer
code: import { readFileSync, writeFileSync, mkdirSync, readdirSync } from 'fs';
// All routes to prerender (generated from App.jsx router config)
// Add all video routes from remaikeData

step3_build_script_update.description: Update package.json build script to include prerendering after Vite build

scripts.build:seo: vite build && vite preview --port 4173 & sleep 3 && node scripts/prerender.mjs && kill %1

step4_fix_index_html_cache_headers.description: REMOVE the anti-caching meta tags from index.html - these are the #1 reason Google may refuse to cache
step4_fix_index_html_cache_headers.notes: Cache headers should be set via server/CDN config, not HTML meta tags. For Vercel/Netlify/Cloudflare Pages, use their caching config instead.

* Sets OG + Twitter meta tags dynamically.

step2_create_og_image.notes: Design: Dark background (#0a0a0a), frai.tv logo centered, tagline 'FREE AI Enhanced TV', gold accent (#c9a962). Can be generated with any image tool.

step3_per_page_og_tags.description: Each page that uses useMeta should also call useOgTags from src/lib/useSEO.js

code: const { remaikeVideos, CATEGORIES, DECADES } = await import('../src/data/remaikeData.js');

scripts.build:seo: vite build && node scripts/generate-sitemap.mjs && vite preview --port 4173 & sleep 3 && node scripts/prerender.mjs && kill %1

strategy.principle: Every YouTube video pushes traffic to frai.tv. Every frai.tv page sends authority signals back to YouTube. Google sees both as a unified media presence.

description_template.first_line: Watch in cinema quality on frai.tv: https://frai.tv/watch/{ytId}
description_template.reasoning: First line of YouTube description appears in search results. Direct link to frai.tv gets clicks and sends referral traffic.

pinned_comment.template: This film is also available on frai.tv in our curated cinema experience: https://frai.tv/watch/{ytId}
pinned_comment.notes: Pinned comments with links improve CTR and send traffic to frai.tv

youtube_to_fraitv.end_screen: Add frai.tv link as an associated website end screen element on every video
youtube_to_fraitv.channel_banner: Add frai.tv URL to channel banner and About section
youtube_to_fraitv.cards: Add YouTube info cards at key moments linking to frai.tv

video_detail_page_changes.description: Add prominent 'Watch on YouTube' button to VideoDetailPage.jsx
code_snippet: // Add after the Share button in VideoDetailPage.jsx actions section
className="btn btn-secondary text-fluid-sm px-3 xs:px-5 py-2.5 xs:py-3"

subscribe_cta.description: Add YouTube subscribe button/CTA in footer or sidebar

playlist_mirroring.description: Create a /series/{seriesId} page for each YouTube playlist
implementation: Fetch playlist data from YouTube API or maintain a static playlist map in remaikeData.js
Each series page shows all episodes in order, linking to both frai.tv /watch/ and YouTube

embed_strategy.description: YouTube embeds on frai.tv count as YouTube views, boosting watch time
embed_strategy.notes: The current VideoPlayer already uses YouTube embeds via iframe - this is correct. Each frai.tv view = YouTube view.

seo_flywheel_mechanics: 1. YouTube description links to frai.tv -> Google discovers frai.tv pages via YouTube crawl
2. frai.tv has proper Schema.org VideoObject -> Google understands video content
3. Video sitemap with video: extensions -> Google Video Search indexes frai.tv pages
4. frai.tv pages link back to YouTube -> Backlink authority flows both ways
5. User searches 'classic film remastered 4K' -> finds frai.tv in Google -> watches on frai.tv -> YouTube embed counts as YouTube view -> boosts YouTube ranking
6. User finds YouTube video -> clicks frai.tv link in description -> browses more content -> increases frai.tv engagement signals

phase6_robots_txt_improvements.title: robots.txt Refinements
phase6_robots_txt_improvements.currentStatus: Good - AI crawlers blocked, main paths allowed

add_video_sitemap_reference.description: If a separate video sitemap is created, add it to robots.txt

block_internal_routes.reasoning: Watchlist and history are user-specific pages with no public content

remove_crawl_delay.description: Remove Crawl-delay directive - it is not respected by Googlebot and may slow down Bing crawling unnecessarily for a small site
remove_crawl_delay.notes: Crawl-delay is non-standard. For a site with ~450 pages, there is no risk of overloading.

phase7_performance_and_caching.title: Performance, Cache Headers, and Preload Hints

step1_remove_anticache_meta.description: Already covered in phase1 step4 - remove no-cache/no-store from index.html

step2_server_cache_headers.description: Set proper Cache-Control headers at the server/CDN level

step3_preload_hints.description: Add resource hints to index.html for critical assets

step4_image_optimization.description: Add loading='lazy' and decoding='async' to thumbnail images across components

week1: Phase 1: Remove anti-cache meta tags from index.html (30 min)

Phase 3 Step 1: Add default OG tags to index.html (30 min)
Phase 2 Step 1: Create useSEO.js helper (1 hour)
Phase 2 Step 2: Add VideoObject JSON-LD to VideoDetailPage (1 hour)
week2: Phase 4: Build sitemap generator script (3 hours)
Phase 1: Build Playwright prerender script (4 hours)
Phase 7: Server cache headers + preload hints (2 hours)
week3: Phase 5: Update YouTube descriptions with frai.tv links (ongoing, batch via API)
Phase 5: Add YouTube button to VideoDetailPage (1 hour)
Phase 3 Step 2: Create OG image (1 hour)
Phase 3 Step 3: Add per-page OG tags to all pages (2 hours)
ongoing: Monitor Google Search Console for indexation progress
Submit sitemap to Google Search Console
Track referral traffic YouTube -> frai.tv and frai.tv -> YouTube
Update YouTube descriptions for new uploads automatically

success_metrics.indexation: Target: 100+ pages indexed within 4 weeks of deployment
success_metrics.video_rich_results: Target: VideoObject rich results appearing for at least 50 videos in Google
success_metrics.referral_traffic: Target: 5% of YouTube traffic clicking through to frai.tv within 8 weeks
success_metrics.og_previews: Target: All shared links show proper thumbnail + title on social media
success_metrics.search_console_coverage: Target: 0 coverage errors, all submitted URLs indexed

src/pages/VideoDetailPage.jsx - add JSON-LD + OG tags + YouTube link button
public/robots.txt - add watchlist/history disallow, remove crawl-delay
